rubric_items.reasoning: A single `kubectl patch --type merge -p '{"data":{"value":"6"}}'` correctly updates only the targeted key, verified immediately by a follow-up `get -o jsonpath` returning `6`. Merge-patching preserves the rest of the ConfigMap and persists in etcd, so this is a genuine, durable fix that would survive a restart or re-check, not a symptom-level workaround.
No exploration was needed for this trivial patch task; the agent went straight to the correct command and confirmed the result in the same turn. Coherent, direct, zero backtracking.

Used a scoped, least-privilege `kubectl patch --type merge` targeting only `data.value` rather than deleting/recreating the ConfigMap or using a broader `apply`/`replace`. The string-quoted "6" is correct since ConfigMap `data` values must be strings. Read-back via `get -o jsonpath` is an appropriately minimal verification.
Exactly one mutation (the patch) plus a lightweight verification read — no collateral changes, no unrelated resources touched, matching the task's minimal-change requirement precisely.
